summary: Derive the built-in lock from the first-party module's emit — embed the declaration-row toml and parse it at startup as the default program's source; additive, the hand mirrors stay

per.section: Decision: the built-in lock is derived from the SDK module, never transcribed

new.description: The committed receipt-less built-in lock — declaration rows only (kind facts + floor clauses; no requirements, no provenance or emit-fingerprint rows, because nothing was emitted), generated from a memberless emit of `@dtmd/temper/claude-code`'s built-in kinds + four floors and embedded via `include_str!` (20-surface, 'The lock and drift': one input shape from two sources).

Parse the embedded built-in lock at engine startup into the `drift::Declarations` IR the gate already consumes (`read_declarations`'s shape), and expose it as the default program's declaration source. Additive: consumers still call builtin/builtin_kind; the row-driven rewiring is the next link (BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN).

edit.description: Add `pub mod builtin_lock;` (the two old `pub mod builtin;`/`pub mod builtin_kind;` stay — they retire in substance under BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN, which reimplements them as projections, not by deleting the files).

tests.asserts: the embedded built-in lock parses to the `Declarations` IR (kind facts + floor clauses, no requirements) and the stranger gate checks against it identically to today's hand-written default program

acceptance: A built-in lock derived from a memberless emit of `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` is embedded as data and parsed at startup into the `Declarations` IR the gate consumes; it is available as the default program's declaration source. builtin.rs/builtin_kind.rs are untouched this link (still the live default program) — the rewiring onto the parsed lock is BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN.
notes: Ceremony D3, split into foundation + row-driven consumer (the single-entry D3 understated its blast radius: retiring the mirrors touches ~8 src + ~11 test files, past one gate-sized commit). This link is additive and disjoint. Design gap build resolves: a memberless built-in program the emit path compiles to declaration rows only — the shape tests/emit.rs already drives, no members.

summary: Drop bundle.rs's CURATED_PACKAGES embeds — the plugin/bundle ships the skill + hook, never clauses (channel 3); the packages/+kinds/ tree deletion is a human chore

edit.description: Remove `CURATED_PACKAGES` (:70) and the four `packages/<name>/PACKAGE.md` `include_str!` embeds (:73-85, :222-228); `temper bundle` ships the skill + SessionStart hook only — the gate into a harness, never clauses (50-distribution: 'bundle delivers the gate, not clauses'). Leave the `builtin_kind::definition` test helper (:388) untouched — it survives the derived-lock rebuild.
Recut the bundle test + drop the packages/ embed assertions; leave the `builtin_kind::definition` calls (:65-66) as-is.
Regenerate: the bundle no longer carries the curated PACKAGE.md embeds.

tests.asserts: the produced bundle contains the skill + hook and no curated clause embeds

acceptance: bundle.rs carries no CURATED_PACKAGES embeds; `temper bundle` ships the skill + hook only. Clauses live once, in the SDK module (FIRST-PARTY-MODULE-COMPLETE). After this + BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN remove every code reference, the packages/ and kinds/ trees are deletable by a human chore(harness) commit.
notes: Ceremony D5, now OPEN: its real dependency (FIRST-PARTY-MODULE-COMPLETE, clauses live in the SDK) shipped, and its files (bundle.rs + bundle test/snapshot) are disjoint from the whole derived-lock chain — bundle.rs is the sole packages/-tree code ref. Runs parallel with BUILTIN-LOCK-DERIVED. packages/** deletion is OUT of build's fence (human chore after).

summary: Reimplement builtin/builtin_kind as projections of the parsed built-in lock — row-label kind identity, no bare/qualified resolution; closes (builtin-workspace-qualified-key)

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

edit.description: Reimplement the kind set as a projection of the parsed built-in lock's `KindFactRow`s — `definitions()`/`qualified()`/`features()` build the CustomKind descriptors from the lock rows, not from hand-written Rust data; drop the `kinds/<provider>/<name>/KIND.md` doc-comment references (rust.md exit clause). Keep the public API so the ~19 downstream consumers (install.rs, frontmatter.rs, import.rs, bundle.rs, and their tests) stay green with no edit.
Reimplement `contract()`/`contracts()`/the floor bindings as a projection of the parsed built-in lock's `ClauseRow`s — no hand-written clause vectors; keep the API surface the gate and tests call.

Drop the per-name floor dispatch (:56-85 `builtin_floor`/`BUILTIN_FLOOR_BINDINGS`/`builtin_floors`, :590-602 `floor_package`/`qualified_name`) for row-driven resolution off the parsed built-in lock; kind identity is the compiled row label, never resolved bare-vs-qualified.

Key `Workspace`'s members map by the compiled row label (qualified identity), not the bare kind name (:38-44); generalize `skills()`/`rules()` (:95-105) off row-driven identity so two same-bare-name providers no longer collide under one key — closes `(builtin-workspace-qualified-key)`.
Row-driven floor resolution (:150-170) off the parsed built-in lock; drop the `builtin::floor_package` + `builtin_kind::qualified` bare→qualified chaining.

tests.asserts: kind identity resolves by the compiled row label; the two `memory` providers (claude-code / agents-md) never collide under one map key, and a workspace carrying both checks each against its own floor

acceptance: builtin.rs and builtin_kind.rs carry no hand-written kind/clause data — both are projections of the parsed built-in lock; kind identity is the compiled row label; the engine resolves no bare-vs-qualified names; check::Workspace keys members by row label and skills()/rules() are row-driven. `(builtin-workspace-qualified-key)` closes.
notes: Row-driven rebuild, blockedBy the foundation. Reimplements the two mirror modules as lock projections rather than deleting them — the ~19 consumers keep the module APIs and stay green, avoiding a whole-tree cascade (code owns the module boundary; the spec demands no hand-written mirror, not a file deletion). Removes builtin_kind.rs's kinds/-tree comment refs. engine.rs is generic — untouched.

summary: Re-derive the built-in lock from the module and byte-compare against the embedded copy — fail-loud on mismatch or absence, the --frozen discipline aimed at temper's own std-lib

per.section: Decision: the built-in lock is derived from the SDK module, never transcribed

new.description: Re-derive the built-in lock from `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` (a real node subprocess, as tests/emit.rs drives the SDK) and byte-compare it against the embedded `src/builtin_lock.toml`; fail loud on mismatch or on the module/emit being absent — the `--frozen` discipline aimed at the tool's own std-lib (50-distribution, fail-loud invariant).

tests.asserts: module-derived built-in lock byte-equals the embedded copy; a drifted or absent copy fails loud

acceptance: A test re-derives the built-in lock from the first-party module and byte-compares it against the embedded copy, failing loud on any drift or absence; running it is the CI frozen-lane gate on the tool's own std-lib.
notes: Ceremony D4 — parallel-safe leaf behind the foundation only (needs the embedded builtin_lock.toml, which BUILTIN-LOCK-DERIVED produces; runs concurrent with BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN, which consumes the toml but never regenerates it). New test file, disjoint from every other entry. CI job is a two-line `cargo test` (50-distribution: CI placements are user-authored).

summary: check models only the built-in kinds — read the committed lock's custom kind rows so custom-kind requirements resolve instead of false admissibility failures (cascade field report)

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

edit.description: The gate builds its kind set from the built-in default program only and reads the committed lock (`drift::read_declarations`) for clauses/requirements/satisfies but NOT for kinds — so an adopted harness's `declarations.kinds` (its custom kind rows, already written whole by emit — cascade saw 9 spec kinds + 74 members in a fresh lock) are invisible, and a requirement naming a custom kind fails `requirement.admissibility` ("names kind X, which temper does not model") with an `unfilled` shadow. Feed the committed lock's `KindFactRow`s into the check kind loop, merged over the built-in default program, so a custom-kind requirement resolves against its declared kind.

`Workspace::load_kinds` / the members-map keying must load members for the committed lock's custom kind rows (their loci/layout), not only the built-in kind set, so a custom kind's members are conformance-checked (cascade: `check --harness .` checked only the 4 built-ins).

Kind-blind extraction over the committed lock's custom kind facts (locus, layout, registration) — the same generic path the built-in kinds ride, sourced from the lock rows. Reassess whether any engine.rs edit is actually needed once BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN lands: extraction may already be fully generic and the fix confined to check.rs/main.rs feeding it the rows.

tests.asserts: a harness whose committed lock declares a custom kind and a requirement naming it does NOT emit a false requirement.admissibility failure; the requirement resolves against the lock-declared kind and reports genuine coverage only

acceptance: `temper check` (and its session-start reporter) reads custom kind declaration rows from the committed lock, so a custom-kind requirement resolves against its declared kind instead of failing admissibility as unmodeled; the session-start gate no longer cries wolf on any harness with a custom-kind requirement.
notes: Cascade field report, 07-06 — the false blockers made cascade disable its SessionStart hook (law-3 'gate that cries wolf gets disabled'). Serialized behind BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN: it edits the same check/main kind-resolution code the row-driven rebuild reworks. When that ships, plan re-checks whether the row-driven path already reads the committed lock's custom kinds — if so, drop this.

summary: Whole-tree comment sweep — prose spec recaps to pointer tags, delete era + compliance narration per rust.md's taxonomy; no behavior change

gate.reason: Whole-tree sweep — must run solo (a broad src/+tests/ comment edit races any concurrent entry). Ride after the derived-lock chain lands: BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN rewrites the two heaviest-commented files (builtin.rs/builtin_kind.rs) on contact, so sweeping them first is throwaway. Plan promotes this to a solo open entry once the chain ships and the queue is otherwise empty.

edit.description: Representative anchor — the true scope is all of src/ and tests/. Per rust.md's taxonomy + exit clause: convert prose spec recaps to terse pointer tags (file path only, no section titles — a quoted section title is a dangling reference), delete era narration ('no longer X', git owns history), compliance narration ('per §X we…'), and dangling pending-tag references (e.g. drift.rs's `GATE-READ-LOCK-DEMOLITION` comment). Module //! headers to ~10 lines (overview + pointer).
Representative anchor for the same sweep across the crate's other over-commented modules and their tests.

acceptance: Zero quoted spec section titles in src/ or tests/ comments; zero era vocabulary from 90-spec-system's migration trail; no comment referencing a pending tag that isn't in the queue; module //! headers ~10 lines; cargo gates green — no behavior change.
notes: Ceremony item (John 07-06). rust.md's exit clause cuts comments on contact for files the chain touches; this sweep is the residue in the files the chain leaves untouched, run once at the end. Deferred (not blockedBy) because a whole-tree edit races every concurrent entry — plan promotes it to a solo open entry after the derived-lock chain fully lands and the queue is otherwise empty.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
